patches.find: at the equatorial ring (r = 14,105 km), n ≈ 1.40. At the ice wall (r = 20,015 km), n ≈ 3.49. At r = 40,000 km, n ≈ 28.8.
patches.replace: at the equatorial ring (r = 14,105 km), n ≈ 1.83. At the ice wall (r = 20,015 km), n ≈ 2.84. At r = 40,000 km (beyond disc geometry), n ≈ 21.53. (The dome's own Height Table lists different values — 1.40, 3.49, 28.8 — which are inconsistent with the above formula and appear to derive from an earlier coefficient of ~0.27; this formula-vs-table discrepancy is documented in Section 2.9.)
patches.rationale: Part 1 currently states the formula with coefficient 0.20, then cites n values that come from the dome's Height Table (coefficient ~0.27) rather than from evaluating the formula. This contradicts Part 2 Section 2.9 which correctly identifies the discrepancy. Curmudgeon flagged as MAJOR (ISS-672-nr-coefficient review 2026-04-10). Fix: cite formula outputs and explicitly flag the table discrepancy.

patches.find: n(r) reaching 28.8 at the disc edge
patches.replace: n(r) reaching 21.53 at extreme southern radii beyond the disc geometry (at the ice wall, r = 20,015 km, the formula gives n ≈ 2.84)
patches.rationale: Part 7 claims n=28.8 at 'the disc edge'. Two errors: (a) disc edge = ice wall at r=20,015 where formula gives n=2.84, not 28.8; (b) n=28.8 (old coefficient) or n=21.53 (current coefficient 0.20) only occurs at r=40,000 which is beyond the dome's disc geometry. Curmudgeon flagged as MAJOR. Fix: cite correct geometry and current formula values.

patches.find: See also CAT-005 / EXP-050 language on verdict criteria for the V51.1 refraction cascade, which uses the same timestamping bar.
patches.rationale: Broken self-anchor: href='#posthoc-standard' points to the section's own h3 id, so clicking bounces the reader back to the top of the same section. Per curmudgeon Cycle 2 review SEC-3.1b.c2.json (CRITICAL), remove this sentence entirely until Section 1.8 (Refraction Cascade) exists with a distinct anchor. The sentence also incorrectly anticipates EXP-050 prose integration that has not yet happened.
